g.text: Application code using generic runtime APIs,
main() function initializes the runtime and sets up producers/consumers.
Abstraction layer defining generic traits and interfaces for COM API.
It includes concept traits for communication backends and
generated interface-specific types for consumers and producers.
It is independent of any specific runtime implementation, and interface oriented.
This contains the concrete LoLa runtime implementation,
This layer is responsible for all runtime operations of lola specific logic.
It maintains the runtime lifecycle and resource management.
Rust-C++ FFI bridge layer that facilitates communication between Rust runtime and C++ middleware.
It contains the necessary bindings and type registry to enable seamless interoperability between the two languages.
Rust code in the runtime layer can invoke C++ middleware functions through this bridge, and C++ code can call back into Rust as needed.
Exports the C++ COM API as a Rust FFI compatible API.
Contains generated C++ code for type exports,
FFI support macros and type registry for managing Rust-C++ interactions,
And it calls the Core C++ middleware API for COM operations.
